original_description: Delegate long-horizon, sandboxed, or context-heavy tasks to Terraphim's Recursive Language Model (RLM) via the local MCP server. Use whenever the user asks to "use RLM", "spawn a sandbox", "recurse over a large codebase/dataset", needs deterministic VM-isolated code or shell execution, has a task that would obviously blow the context window, or invokes /rlm. Prefer this over inline shell loops when isolation, budget tracking, or recursive LLM calls would help. Wraps the rlm_code, rlm_bash, rlm_query, rlm_context, rlm_snapshot, and rlm_status MCP tools shipped by the terraphim_rlm crate in terraphim-ai. Do not use for one-shot edits (call Edit/Read/Bash directly), PR review (prefer code-review or structural-pr-review), or personal notes lookup (prefer local-knowledge).
best_description: Delegate long-horizon, sandboxed, or context-heavy tasks to Terraphim's Recursive Language Model (RLM) via the local MCP server. Use whenever the user asks to "use RLM", "spawn a sandbox", "recurse over a large codebase/dataset", needs deterministic VM-isolated code or shell execution, has a task that would obviously blow the context window, or invokes /rlm. Prefer this over inline shell loops when isolation, budget tracking, or recursive LLM calls would help. Wraps the rlm_code, rlm_bash, rlm_query, rlm_context, rlm_snapshot, and rlm_status MCP tools shipped by the terraphim_rlm crate in terraphim-ai. Do not use for one-shot edits (call Edit/Read/Bash directly), PR review (prefer code-review or structural-pr-review), or personal notes lookup (prefer local-knowledge).
final_description: Use this skill when a task is too big, too risky, or too recursive for one Claude context. Trigger whenever the user wants to: fan out analysis across a large monorepo/codebase/dataset and stitch results back together, decompose work into planner→sub-model→reconciler passes, run untrusted or sandboxed code in a VM/container ("dark factory", isolation, scraper, untrusted input), track an explicit token/cost budget with stop-on-overrun, or survive an overnight/long-horizon job without context blowup. Trigger on phrases like "RLM", "recursive language model", "/rlm", "spawn a sandbox", "fan out per crate/file", "split into sub-queries", "recurse over", "budget-tracked", "context would blow up", "60 crates", "300 files", "isolate this script", "VM/Firecracker/Docker executor". Skip for one-shot edits or questions (Edit/Read/Bash), PR review (code-review, structural-pr-review), personal-notes lookup (local-knowledge), and plain background jobs without recursion or isolation (adf-orchestrate).

history.description: Delegate long-horizon, sandboxed, or context-heavy tasks to Terraphim's Recursive Language Model (RLM) via the local MCP server. Use whenever the user asks to "use RLM", "spawn a sandbox", "recurse over a large codebase/dataset", needs deterministic VM-isolated code or shell execution, has a task that would obviously blow the context window, or invokes /rlm. Prefer this over inline shell loops when isolation, budget tracking, or recursive LLM calls would help. Wraps the rlm_code, rlm_bash, rlm_query, rlm_context, rlm_snapshot, and rlm_status MCP tools shipped by the terraphim_rlm crate in terraphim-ai. Do not use for one-shot edits (call Edit/Read/Bash directly), PR review (prefer code-review or structural-pr-review), or personal notes lookup (prefer local-knowledge).

train_results.query: I need recursive LLM calls -- the outer model should plan, then call cheaper sub-models for each step, then a final reconciliation pass. Use whatever Terraphim has for this.
Use the dark factory to run this untrusted scraper script in isolation -- I don't trust the input HTML and don't want it touching my machine outside a VM.
ok so this rust workspace has like 60 crates and every time i try to analyse it claude eats the context, can you split the work into sandboxed sub-queries and stitch the answers back together
Generate a 5-line bash one-liner that finds all *.lock files modified in the last 24 hours and prints them sorted by size.
I've got a 300-file Rust monorepo and need to produce per-crate ownership summaries plus a dependency cycle report. Decompose this with RLM, fan out per crate, reconcile back. Budget is tight.
Ingest the conclusions from this debugging session into my Rust Engineer knowledge graph so I can find them later.
Add a TODO comment on line 42 of src/lib.rs that says 'fixme: bounds check'.
Wrap this overnight analysis in a budget-tracked recursive session -- if it goes over 50k tokens I want it to stop and ask, not silently truncate.
Kick off the meta-learning agent on bigbox to run overnight, I'll check the status in the morning.
Format this Rust file with rustfmt and tell me what changed.
I want to compare three reviewer perspectives -- security, performance, correctness -- on this big migration PR. Adversarial swarm please.
What's the difference between `Arc<Mutex<T>>` and `Arc<RwLock<T>>` in tokio? Two paragraphs is fine.
How do I write an integration test in tokio that mocks an HTTP server? Show me the boilerplate.

Open the README.md and find the install instructions. Quote them back to me.

history.description: Use this skill for tasks exceeding a single context window, needing sandboxed/VM-isolated execution, recursive LLM decomposition (planner calls sub-models then reconciles), or explicit token-budget tracking with stop-on-overrun. Trigger on: analysing large monorepos/codebases/datasets by fanning out per-subunit and stitching results, running untrusted or risky code in isolation, multi-stage plan→execute→reconcile pipelines, budget-bounded long-horizon analyses, "dark factory"/sandbox/VM execution requests, fan-out/fan-in or map-reduce LLM workflows, and any mention of RLM, recursive language model, /rlm, or Terraphim's recursive sandbox. Also trigger when the user worries about context rot/blowup or wants deterministic replayable orchestration of many LLM calls. Skip for: one-shot edits or reads (Edit/Read/Bash), PR review (code-review or structural-pr-review), personal notes (local-knowledge), and simple background jobs without recursion or isolation (adf-orchestrate).
